Title: Inverse regulation of SOS1 and HKT1 protein localization and stability by SOS3/CBL4 in Arabidopsis thaliana
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: osmotic adjustment in plants; excessive accumulation causes ion toxicity under salinity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root and shoot vascular transport stream; root tissues and shoots
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plasma membrane Na+/H+ antiporter (CPA1/NHX family); cyclic-nucleotide-binding homologous domain; auto-inhibitory C-terminal domain; SOS3-binding domain
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane; intracellular compartments; vacuole-associated signal upon mislocalization in sos3 mutant
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional analysis focused on root meristematic/epidermal cells and differentiated stele under salt stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Na+ efflux from roots and Na+ loading into xylem; requires SOS3 binding for salt-induced plasma membrane recruitment and in planta function
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC; co-immunoprecipitation; yeast salt-tolerance complementation; transgenic complementation in sos1-1; confocal localization under salt treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress (50–100 mM NaCl); sodicity regimes with 5 mM Na+ and varying K+
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SOS1 directly interacts with SOS3 via a newly mapped SOS3-binding domain (K460-L482). 2. Deletion of this domain abolishes SOS3 interaction, prevents proper plasma membrane recruitment in planta, and fails to complement sos1-1 salt sensitivity. 3. SOS1 catalytic activation is mainly driven by SOS2/SOS3 phosphorylation, whereas direct SOS3 binding is decisive for trafficking/localization under salinity.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes salt hypersensitivity and excessive root Na+ accumulation; deletion of SOS3-binding domain alters basal activity and mislocalization
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although mechanistically a transporter, not an enzyme or TF, SOS1 is one of the two principal antagonistic Na+ transport systems examined in Results and is therefore a core common gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: osmotic adjustment in plants; protection from shoot Na+ toxicity requires controlled distribution
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: xylem sap retrieval pathway affecting root-to-shoot Na+ partition
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HKT family Na+ transporter/channel-like protein; class-I HKT
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low and localized expression in differentiated vascular tissues; native-expression complementation line used
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediates Na+ unloading/retrieval from xylem; under acute salt or high Ca2+ stress the protein is degraded in a SOS3-dependent, MG132-sensitive manner, reducing Na+ retrieval and favoring export to shoot
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC; co-immunoprecipitation; Xenopus oocyte transport assay; western blot time course in 35S and native-promoter lines; MG132 inhibition; genetic analysis of hkt1 and double mutants
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress (100 mM NaCl); high CaCl2 (3 mM); MG132 proteasome inhibitor; sodicity treatments with 5 mM Na+ and varying K+
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HKT1;1 directly interacts with SOS3 through a cytosolic loop region between residues 104 and 152. 2. Salt and high Ca2+ trigger HKT1;1 degradation through an MG132-sensitive pathway. 3. This degradation requires SOS3 but not SOS1 or SOS2, and reduces Na+ unloading from xylem under acute stress.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of HKT1;1 increases shoot Na+ under milder conditions; acute stress-induced degradation likely compromises Na+ retrieval but helps avoid harmful root Na+ retention
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: NA in this study; discussion cites promoter methylation as prior knowledge only
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport activity in Xenopus oocytes was not altered by SOS3 co-expression, indicating the main SOS3 effect is on protein stability rather than channel conductance.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: osmotic adjustment in plants; excess causes salinity toxicity, so controlled partitioning supports salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root-to-shoot transport axis via root plasma membrane efflux and xylem loading/unloading processes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Calcineurin B-like calcium sensor (CBL4/SOS3) with EF-hand Ca2+-binding motifs; N-myristoylation site at Gly2
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Salt stress; acute sodicity stress; elevated cytosolic Ca2+; exogenous CaCl2 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane-associated; also reported as stabilized upon salt/Ca2+ treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a molecular switch that promotes SOS1 plasma membrane recruitment and activation while triggering HKT1;1 degradation, thereby shifting Na+ flux from root retention toward root export/xylem loading under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC; co-immunoprecipitation; synthetic peptide binding assay; confocal localization of SOS1 in wild type vs sos3 mutants; mutant/complementation genetics; western blot assays for HKT1;1 degradation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress (50–100 mM NaCl); acute sodicity; high CaCl2 (3 mM); MG132; low-K+/Na+ hydroponic treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SOS3 directly binds previously unrecognized functional domains in both SOS1 and HKT1;1. 2. SOS3 is required for salt-induced recruitment of SOS1 to the plasma membrane and for subsequent in planta SOS1 function. 3. SOS3 is also required for salt- and Ca2+-induced, proteasome-sensitive degradation of HKT1;1, coordinating opposing Na+ transport systems.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function analyzed mainly in root tissues under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master switch coordinating the two principal Na+ transport systems that determine plant Na+ partition under salinity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes SOS1 plasma membrane recruitment and facilitates its activation with SOS2; promotes HKT1;1 protein degradation under acute salt/Ca2+ stress
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Na+ transport homeostasis; transporter trafficking/localization; transporter protein stability; long-distance ion partitioning
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct protein binding; calcium-dependent signaling; membrane recruitment; promotion of proteasome-dependent degradation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms SOS2/SOS3 kinase complex for SOS1 activation; also binds SOS1 and HKT1;1 independently of SOS2
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of SOS3 causes salt hypersensitivity and excessive root Na+ retention; non-myristoylated SOS3 cannot support HKT1;1 degradation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value salinity-tolerance target for engineering coordinated Na+ partitioning. Strategies may include enhancing stress-activated SOS3 stability or membrane recruitment, while preserving conditional regulation to avoid excessive shoot Na+ loading under mild salinity.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter; native genomic context in mutants/complementation lines
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SOS3-1 mutant with defective EF-hand cannot bind SOS1 or HKT1;1. PM targeting via N-myristoylation is required for HKT1;1 degradation.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: osmotic adjustment in plants; controlled Na+ export and partition improve salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root plasma membrane and xylem-loading system via SOS1 regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Salt stress via SOS3/CBL4 calcium sensor complex
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane when recruited by SOS3/CBL4 complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates SOS1 by phosphorylation but is not required for SOS3-mediated HKT1;1 degradation and does not mediate SOS3 direct binding to SOS1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast reconstitution assays; interaction comparisons; mutant analysis of HKT1;1 degradation in sos2-2 background
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SOS2 is part of the canonical SOS2/SOS3 kinase complex that activates SOS1. 2. SOS3 binding to SOS1 is SOS2-independent. 3. Salt-induced HKT1;1 degradation still occurs in sos2-2, showing SOS2 is not essential for that branch of regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important activator of SOS1-mediated Na+ efflux, but not the central switch in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Na+ efflux/xylem loading through SOS1 activation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful supporting target for salinity engineering when coordinated with SOS3 and SOS1, though not the main discovery focus here.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulatory gene because Results directly test its necessity relative to SOS3 for SOS1 activation versus HKT1;1 degradation.
